title: Proclamation No. 380, s. 1957
text:
MALACAÑANG

RESIDENCE OF THE PRESIDENT OF THE PHILIPPINES MANILA

PROCLAMATION NO. 380

RESERVING FOR CAMP SITES PURPOSES CERTAIN PARCELS OF THE PRIVATE DOMAIN OF THE GOVERNMENT SITUATED IN THE MUNICIPALITY OF BONGABON, PROVINCE OF NUEVA ECIJA, ISLAND OF LUZON

Upon recommendation of the Secretary of Agriculture and Natural Resources and pursuant to the provisions of section 64 ( e ) of the Revised Administrative Code, I, Ramon Magsaysay, President of the Philippines, do hereby withdraw from sale or settlement and reserve for camp sites purposes of the Boy Scouts and Girl Scouts of the Philippines, under the respective administration of the said organizations, subject to private rights, if any there be, certain parcels of the private domain of the Government situated in the Municipality of Bongabon, province of Nueva Ecija, island of Luzon, and more particularly described in the Bureau of Lands plan Bsd-11695, to wit:

Lot 1-A-2, Bsd-11695 (Camp site of the Girl Scouts of the Philippines)

A parcel of land (lot 1-A-2 of the subdivision plan Bsd-11695, being a portion of lot 1-A described on plan Bsd-10908 G.L.R.O. record No. ‑‑‑‑‑), situated in the barrio of Atate, municipality of Bongabon, province of Nueva Ecija. Bounded on the SE., along lines 1-2 and 2-3 by provincial road; along line 3-4 by lot l-A-3 of the subdivision plan; and along line 4-1 by lot R of the subdivision plan. Beginning at a point marked 1 on plan, being N. 62° 53′ E., 15,771.77 meters from B.L.L.M. 1, Cabanatuan cadastre, thence S. 25° 39′ W., 127.60 meters to point 2; thence S. 38° 29′ W., 519.38 meters to point 3; thence N. 57° 39′ W., 65.38 meters to point 4; thence N. 41° 59′ E., 249.12 meters to the point of beginning; containing an area of 27,826 square meters. All points referred to are indicated on the plan and are marked on the ground by B.L. cylindrical concrete monuments; bearing true; date of the subdivision survey, February 28-29 and March 8-9, 1956.

Lot 2-A-2, Bsd-11695 (Camp site, of the Girl Scouts of the Philippines)

A parcel of land (lot 2-A-2 of the .subdivision plan Bsd-11695, being a portion of lot 2-A described on plan Bsd-10908, G.L.R.O. record No. ‑‑‑‑‑), situated in the barrio of Atate, municipality of Bongabon, province of Nueva Ecija. Bounded on the SE., along line 1-2 by lot road lot R of the subdivision plan; along line 2-3 by lot 2-A-3 of the subdivision plan; along line 3-10 by Pampanga River; and along line 10-1 by lot 2-B, Bsd-10908 (Philippine Rural Reconstruction Movement). Beginning at a point marked 1 on plan, being N. 62° 45′ E., 15,816.78 meters from B.L.L.M. 1, Cabanatuan cadastre, thence S. 41° 59′ W., 711.30 meters to point 2; thence N. 57° 55′ W., 314.80 meters to point 3; thence N. 69° 26′ E., 72.42 meters to point 4; thence N. 3° 29′ W., 122.42 meters to point 5; thence N. 22° 47′. E., 76.82 meters to point 6; thence N. 23° 38′ E., 40.40 meters to point 7; thence N. 14° 30′ E., 109.54 meters to point 8; thence N. 24° 53′ E., 59.85 meters to point 9; thence N. 18° 15′ E., 166.04 meters to point 10; thence S. 67° 42′ E., 558.40 meters to the point of beginning; containing an area of 276,191 square meters. All points referred to are indicated on the plan and are marked on the ground as follows: point 3 by old corner; points 4-10, inclusive, by stakes; and the rest by B.L. cylindrical concrete monuments; bearings true; date of the subdivision survey, February 28-29 and March 8-9, 1956.

Lot 1-A-1, Bsd-11695 (Camp site of the Boys Scouts of the Philippines)

A parcel of land (lot 1-A-1 of the subdivision plan Bsd-11695, being a portion of lot 1-A described on plan Bsd-10908, G.L.R.O. record No. ‑‑‑‑‑), situated in the barrio of Atate, municipality of Bongabon, province of Nueva Ecija. Bounded on the NE., along line 1-2 by lot l-A-3 of the subdivision plan; along line 2-3 by provincial road; along 3-4 by lot 1-A-portion, Bsd-10908; and along line 4-1 by lot road lot R of the subdivision plan. Beginning at a point marked 1 on plan, being N. 63° 48′ E., 15,149.24 meters from B.L.L.M. 1, Cabanatuan cadastre, thence S. 57° 34′ E., 66.31 meters to point 2; thence S. 38° 28′ W., 247.93 meters to point 3; thence N. 56° 53′ W., 81.50 meters to point 4; thence N. 41° 59′ E., 249.12 meters to the point of beginning; containing an area of 18,207 square meters. All points referred to are indicated on the plan and are marked on the ground by B.L. cylindrical concrete monuments; bearings true; date of the subdivision survey, February 28-29 and March 8-9, 1956.

Lot 2-A-1, Bsd-11695 (Camp site of the Boy Scouts of the Philippines)

A parcel of land (lot 2-A-1 of the subdivision plan Bsd-11695, being a portion of lot 2-A described on plan Bsd-10908, G.L.R.O. record No. ‑‑‑‑‑), situated in the barrio of Atate, municipality of Bongabon, province of Nueva Ecija. Bounded on the SE., along line 1-2 by lot road lot R of the subdivision plan; along line 2-12 by lot 2-A, Bsd-10908 (Capitol site); along line 12-27 by Tampanga River; and along line 27-1 by lot 2-A-3 of the subdivision plan. Beginning at a point marked 1 on plan, being N. 63° 44′ E., 15,137.24 meters from B.L.L.M. 1, Cabanatuan cadastre, thence S. 41° 59′ W., 248.86 meters to point 2; thence N. 58° 57′ W., 17.36 meters to point 3; thence S. 44° 22′ W., 119.42 meters to point 4; thence N. 55° 46′ W., 107.66 meters to point 5; thence S. 86° 30′ W., 12.45 meters to point 6; thence S. 62° 58′ W., 64.99 meters to point 7; thence N. 32° 13′ W., 63.09 meters, to point 8; thence S. 54° 44′ W., 244.89 meters to point 9; thence S. 56° 51′ W, 85.54 meters to point 10; thence N. 37° 26′ W., 586.15 meters to paint 11; thence N. 67° 58′ E., 50 meters to point 12; thence S. 53° 12′ E., 66.61 meters to point 13; thence N. 80° 35′ E., 46.36 meters to point 14; thence S. 84° 22′ E., 34.16 meters to point 15; thence S. 57° 09′ E., 29.58 meters to point 16; thence S. 52° 23′ E., 106.19 meters to point 17; thence S. 85° 50′ E., 36.29 meters to point;18; thence S. 68° 52′ E., 73.36 meters to point 19; thence S. 81° 39′ E., 45.27 meters to point 20; thence N. 83° 20′ E., 47.34 meters to point 21; thence N. 81° 46′ E., 36.98 meters to point 22; thence Ni. 77° 28′ E., 26.37 meters to point 23; thence N. 67° 25′ E., 141.04 meters to point 24; thence N. 50° 38′ E., 58.28 meters to point 25; thence N. 34° 2V E., 63.40 meters to point 26; thence N. 49° 09′ E., 70.16 meters to point 27; thence S. 57° 54′ E., 323.50 meters to the point of beginning; containing an area of 255,669 square meters. All points referred to are indicated on the plan and are marked on the ground as follows: points 12-26, inclusive, by stakes; point 27 by old corner; and the rest by B.L. cylindrical concrete monuments; bearings true; date of the subdivision survey, February 28-29, and March 8-9, 1956.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, I have hereunto set my hand and caused the seal of the Republic of the Philippines to be affixed.

Done in the City of Manila, this 11th day of January, in the year of Our Lord, nineteen hundred and fifty-seven, and of the Independence of the Philippines, the eleventh.

RAMON MAGSAYSAY President of the Philippines

By the President:

FORTUNATO DE LEON Executive Secretary

Source: Ateneo Professional School Library
